text.title: AARRR Pirate Metrics Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding AARRR Pirate Metrics. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each of 5 stages (acquisition, activation, retention, revenue, referral) MUST have ≥1 numeric metric + a 30-day baseline.

rationale.source: Dave McClure original AARRR + Reforge growth model

rule.rationale: Stages without baselines can't be bottleneck-diagnosed; baseline-less reports are vanity.

rule.statement: Funnel MUST be analyzed top-down. Optimization on stage N is forbidden while stage <N has an unexplained drop >30%.
rule.rationale: Optimizing downstream of a leaky upstream stage is wasted effort.

rule.statement: Before any tactic is queued, the bottleneck stage MUST be explicitly named. Tactics without a stage tag are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without a named bottleneck, tactic selection becomes politics.

rule.statement: Weekly dashboard MUST publish all 5 stages even when only 1 is the focus. Single-stage dashboards hide regression elsewhere.
rule.rationale: Pinning all 5 makes silent regressions visible.

rule.statement: Each tactic in the backlog MUST be tagged with exactly ONE stage it moves. Cross-stage tactics get split.
rule.rationale: Tactics that 'move everything' are usually vague; one-stage tag forces specificity.
